stripe/stripe-ios#2671.body:
## Summary

Stripe identity verification SDK crashes for iPads with the following message:

> NSGenericException · Your application has presented a UIAlertController (<UIAlertController: 0x10d0a7000>) of style UIAlertControllerStyleActionSheet from StripeIdentity.IdentityFlowNavigationController (<StripeIdentity.IdentityFlowNavigationController: 0x10d08da00>). The modalPresentationStyle of a UIAlertController with this style is UIModalPresentationPopover. You must provide location information for this popover through the alert controller's popoverPresentationController. You must provide either a sourceView and sourceRect or a barButtonItem. If this information is not known when you present the alert controller, you may provide it in the UIPopoverPresentationControllerDelegate method -prepareForPopoverPresentation.

stripe/stripe-ios#3597.body:
## Summary
We only accept driving licences but in the interface users get confused because the headers are "Front of identity card" and "Back of identity card".

In German this translates to "[Vorderseite des Personalausweises](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/114360f8731414b53c23cacd0c4475b5fcbb4bf6/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Resources/Localizations/de.lproj/Localizable.strings#L35)" which is not a driving license.

See also:
- https://github.com/stripe/stripe-android/issues/8511

## Version
The problem occurs with `stripe-ios` 23.27.2

## Details
We currently only accept driver's licences by defining `options.document.allowed_types = ["driving_license"]` when using the [API](https://docs.stripe.com/api/identity/verification_sessions/create#create_identity_verification_session-options-document-allowed_types)

Available options are:
- `driving_license` ... Drivers license document type.
- `id_card` ... ID card document type.
- `passport` ... Passport document type.

But in the interface the title is always "Front of identity card".

The text is "Position your identity card in the centre of the frame".

In the code you only use "Front of identity card" [here](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/114360f8731414b53c23cacd0c4475b5fcbb4bf6/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Source/NativeComponents/ViewControllers/DocumentCaptureViewController%2BStrings.swift#L15-L25).

![iOS Native - English 2](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/assets/282412/89e6241b-e42b-4fab-a56e-061ce8ca1b56)

---

On the first screen you use:
"Get ready to scan your photo ID"

![iOS Native - English 1](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/assets/282412/e4f846b5-6868-4aea-a40e-eea83ffa4891)
stripe/stripe-ios#3597.title: [BUG] Wrong title "Front of identity card"

stripe/stripe-ios#5000.body:
## Summary
Anything relying on `#if canImport(CompositorServices)` breaks when running on macOS 26 / Catalyst with the following error: 'currency' is only available in iOS 16 or newer

## Code to reproduce
Import Stripe and build on iOS 26. Builds fine
Do the same on Catalyst/macOS you'll get the above error

## iOS version
Catalyst 26

## Installation method
Swift Packages

## SDK version
24.15.0

## Other information

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad94a895-8242-4dfa-a50b-90d89e732246)
stripe/stripe-ios#5000.title: [BUG] 'x' is on available in iOS 16 or newer

stripe/stripe-ios#5269.body: ## Summary When presenting the customer sheet, the onCompletion block call crashes ## What is blocked by this issue? You can't open the customer sheet ## Code to reproduce ## iOS version 26 Beta 4 (this would also happen on prod if using swift 6 or dynamic actor isolation feature flag) ## Installation method SPM ## SDK version 24.19.0 ## Other information It's happening because the onCompletion closure's actor isolation does not match the actor isolation of where it's being called. You can probably fix it by updating the relevant closures like so, and ensuring the completion handler is called from the main actor: ``` public func customerSheet( isPresented: Binding, customerSheet: CustomerSheet, onCompletion: @escaping @MainActor (CustomerSheet.CustomerSheetResult) -> Void ) -> some View { ``` alternatively, you can probably mark them all as nonisolated async. But since this is used in a UI context, main actor probably makes more sense.
stripe/stripe-ios#5269.title: [BUG] [P1] CustomerSheet SwiftUI onCompletion block crashes with Dynamic Actor Isolation feature flag (or swift 6) enabled

stripe/stripe-ios#5300.body:
Copy of https://github.com/stripe/stripe-android/issues/9052:

When users start the Stripe Identity flow on a small screen with large font, they are prompted to "SCROLL TO CONTINUE".

However, we found that most users scroll on the right side of their screen, where the activity is not scrollable. 

Please make the entire screen scrollable to make scrolling easier.
stripe/stripe-ios#5300.title: [BUG] When the "SCROLL TO CONTINUE" button is displayed, the entire screen should be scrollable.

stripe/stripe-ios#5451.body: The upload photo button still is shown even when live capture requirement is requested.
stripe/stripe-ios#5451.title: [BUG] [Identity] Live capture requirement not respected always for document capture

stripe/stripe-ios#5836.body:
## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
The web currentlyhas more actionable feedback for the user when local models are failing than native mobile.

## Describe the solution you'd like

Provide feedback for:

- [ ] When blur detector scores are above a threshold
  - iOS has 2 blur detector models
    - [LaplacianBlurDetector](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/0e0a18c1ea411c00fa37c46336d57fce06230d94/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Source/NativeComponents/Detectors/LaplacianBlurDetector.swift)
    - [MotionBlurDetector](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/0e0a18c1ea411c00fa37c46336d57fce06230d94/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Source/NativeComponents/Detectors/MotionBlurDetector.swift)
- [ ] When [IDDetector](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/0e0a18c1ea411c00fa37c46336d57fce06230d94/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Source/NativeComponents/Detectors/IDDetector/IDDetectorOutput.swift#L21-L24) scores indicate that the wrong side is being shown
  - Note there’s no no_document output on the iOS detector. Instead use the following:
    - no_document = low scores for passport or idCardFront when capturing ID front side, idCardBack when capturing ID back side
- [ ]  If ID is not centered
  - Check if the document location bounding box is centered enough
- [ ]  If IDDetector [invalid score](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/blob/0e0a18c1ea411c00fa37c46336d57fce06230d94/StripeIdentity/StripeIdentity/Source/NativeComponents/Detectors/IDDetector/IDDetectorOutput.swift#L24) is high

## Additional context

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ev3Z1t_XDjIX4coFJaG2ywgsPDz1eYVQv-cCgSCEB30/edit?tab=t.0
stripe/stripe-ios#5836.title: [Feature] More Actionable User Feedback for Document Capture Failures

stripe/stripe-ios#6013.body: ## Summary After upgrading to Swift 6, we are seeing a crash runtime warning when presenting CustomerSheet that results in completion(CustomerSheetResult.error). The warning is `\StripePaymentSheetPresenter.isPaymentMethodsPresented is isolated to the main actor. Accessing it via Binding from a different actor will cause undefined behaviors, and potential data races; This warning will become a runtime crash in a future version of SwiftUI.` CustomerSheet+SwiftUI.swift ## Code to reproduce Present Customer Sheet but provide invalid ephemeral key or setup intent secret, causing completion to be called with Error ## iOS version iOS 26 ## Installation method SPM ## SDK version 25.5.0 ## Other information
stripe/stripe-ios#6013.title: [BUG] CustomerSheet - MainActor isolated crash runtime warning (Swift 6)

stripe/stripe-ios#6037.body:
Add a 1.0 second sampling period after we find a valid document capture frame, instead of picking that first frame, to see if we can capture a better quality frame during that window. If we do, we use that frame instead of the first frame.

Future improvements for analytics:

- [ ] Number of frames we looked at
- [ ] Individual scores
- [ ] Final overall best score
stripe/stripe-ios#6037.title: [Feature] Add Best Frame detector

stripe/stripe-ios#6041.body:
## Summary
<!-- A simple summary of the problems you're having. -->
**Context**: We are using Customer Sheet in our iOS app, implemented using SwiftUI
**Problem**: When you present the sheet, and the user dismiss/closes the sheet while the payment methods are still loading, you are unable to present the sheet again because the binding var is stuck on true
**Technical Dive**: The issue is because the binding var that is bassed in `isPresented` is never reset to `false` if you dismiss while loading (`LoadingViewController`)


## What is blocked by this issue?
<!-- Summarize what the user is unable to do. (e.g. checking out, saving a card, etc.) -->
If user has bad network connectivity while trying to present the Customer Sheet, there is a decent probability they may dismiss the sheet to try again. If so, they will no longer be able to present the sheet unless they kill the app. We use this in our booking/checkout flow so this would be a very bad experience and result in lost conversion

## Code to reproduce
<!-- If possible, please include a brief piece of code (or ideally, a link to an example project) demonstrating the problem you're having. -->
Problem begins in `CustomerSheet+SwiftUI.swift `
`self.parent.presented = false` is never called if you dismiss `LoadingViewController`. Anyone trying to set this to true in a subsequent "present" will see not see any effect 

## iOS version
<!-- What version of iOS are you observing the problem on? -->

## Installation method
<!-- How did you install our SDK? -->
Swift Package Manager

## SDK version
<!--
What version of our SDK are you using? You can find this by either looking at your `Podfile.lock` (if you're using Cocoapods) or by looking at the value of `STPSDKVersion` in `STPAPIClient.h`.
 -->
iOS SDK 25.6.0

## Other information
<!-- Anything else you can include that'll make it easier for us to help you! -->
stripe/stripe-ios#6041.title: [BUG] [P1] CustomerSheet Swift UI - Dismiss while loading causes binding var to be stuck as "true"

stripe/stripe-ios#6057.body: ## Summary We are experiencing a layout issue in the **CustomerSheet**, specifically on the **Add Card** screen. When an input field becomes active and the keyboard is presented, the focused field is not fully visible within the component. A portion of the input field appears to be cut off. This results in a visually inconsistent layout and negatively impacts usability. The clipped appearance makes the interface look slightly broken. The issue consistently occurs when the keyboard is shown. Additionally, after dismissing the keyboard by tapping on the blank area of the sheet, the content does not readjust to its initial layout. The UI remains shifted upward, requiring the user to manually scroll to view all elements (see the second screenshot). Screenshots are attached for reference. ## Code to reproduce This is how I'm presenting the customer sheet. ``` let customerSheet = CustomerSheet( configuration: configuration, intentConfiguration: intentConfiguration, customerSessionClientSecretProvider: { CustomerSessionClientSecret( customerId: customerSession.customer, clientSecret: customerSession.clientSecret ) } ) customerSheet.present(from: viewController) { result in let completion: StripeCustomerSheetCompletion switch result { case .canceled(let paymentOption): completion = .canceled(selection: Self.map(paymentOption)) case .selected(let paymentOption): completion = .selected(selection: Self.map(paymentOption)) case .error(let error): completion = .error(error) } callbacks.onCompletion?(completion) if case .error(let err) = result { callbacks.onError?(err) } callbacks.onDismiss?() } ``` ## iOS version iOS 26.2 iOS 18.5 ## Installation method SPM ## SDK version 25.6.2 ## Other information When the **Customer Sheet** is presented, I'm seeing this warning in Xcode, so I'm wondering if there is some connection with the issue I'm facing. ``` Calling -viewDidAppear: directly on a view controller is not supported, and may result in out-of-order callbacks and other inconsistent behavior. Use the -beginAppearanceTransition:animated: and -endAppearanceTransition APIs on UIViewController to manually drive appearance callbacks instead. Make a symbolic breakpoint at UIViewControllerAlertForAppearanceCallbackMisuse to catch this in the debugger. View controller: ``` ## Screenshots
stripe/stripe-ios#6057.title: [BUG] [CustomerSheet] - Add New Card - Focused Field Layout

stripe/stripe-ios#6095.body: ## Summary We're having trouble submitting our latest iOS release due to a Stripe SDK issue. The AppStore Connect validation failed with: "Invalid bundle: The bundle at 'ANT.app/Frameworks/StripeIssuing.framework' contains disallowed file 'Frameworks'." Looking in the xcframework files, we do see the extra Frameworks folder under StripeIssuing. Any guidance here? ## Code to reproduce We are importing the Stripe SDK as embedded XCFrameworks using binary targets: ``` /// Stripe /// Version 25.6.0 /// https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ios/releases .binaryTarget(name: "Stripe3DS2", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/Stripe3DS2.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripeCore", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripeCore.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripeApplePay", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripeApplePay.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripeIssuing", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripeIssuing.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripeUICore", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripeUICore.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripePayments", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripePayments.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripePaymentsUI", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripePaymentsUI.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "Stripe", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/Stripe.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripePaymentSheet", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripePaymentSheet.xcframework"), .binaryTarget(name: "StripeFinancialConnections", path: "./frameworks/Stripe/StripeFinancialConnections.xcframework"), ``` We are able to build and archive dev builds fine, it's only failing on the submission to App Store Connect. Also, if we delete the embedded Frameworks folder, we are able to submit to the App Store with no issues. ## iOS version n/a ## Installation method Manually dragging in the XCFrameworks ## SDK version 25.6, but also confirmed is still an issue on the latest SDK ## Other information
stripe/stripe-ios#6095.title: [BUG] Failing App Store submissions validations w/ Embedded Framework warnings

stripe/stripe-ios#6267.body:
## Summary
Apple pay treats 1000 as 1000 HUF instead of 10.00.  
This prevents users from paying for anything via Apple Pay in HUF. We noticed a bunch of "amount_too_large" errors in the dashboard. Only happening through apple pay. The payment intent is correctly created, the correct value eg.: 18980.00 HUF shows up in the dashboard aswell. Then when the user pays we get the error on /v1/tokens:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "amount_too_large",
    "doc_url": "https://stripe.com/docs/error-codes/amount-too-large",
    "message": "Az összeg nem lehet több, mint 999,999.99 Ft",
    "request_log_url": "https://dashboard.stripe.com/acct_1T4Tv84KM8ggLuyF/workbench/logs?object=REDACTED",
    "type": "invalid_request_error"
  }
}
```

## What is blocked by this issue?
Payment via Apple Pay

## Code to reproduce
Create any payment intent with HUF above 10000 then try to pay for it with apple pay. Issue observed from **flutter-stripe**

## iOS version
18.3.2

## Installation method
via flutter-stripe

## SDK version
24.7.0 but observed in 25.9.0 aswell

## Other information
Apple Pay treats HUF as zero decimal currency whilst stripe treats it as 2 decimal. I was unable to find a solution for this, in any documentation. My only concern is this seems so critical that not seeing any issues for it makes me think I'm missing something but I validated in a new project and obviously the one in prod is still suffering from this.
stripe/stripe-ios#6267.title: [BUG] [P1]
